title: Add graceful shutdown with preStop hook and startup probe
description:
# GitHub Issue #160: Add graceful shutdown with preStop hook and startup probe

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: The Keystone API Deployment currently has no `preStop` lifecycle hook, no explicit `terminationGracePeriodSeconds`, and no startup probe. This means that during rolling updates or node drains, in-flight requests can be dropped because the pod is removed from the Service endpoints and terminated simultaneously. Additionally, after a cold start (e.g. following a config change or db_sync), the liveness probe with its 15-second initial delay may kill the container before Keystone has finished initializing.

Add the following to the `keystone-api` container in `buildKeystoneDeployment`:

1. **PreStop hook** with a short sleep to allow kube-proxy/iptables to converge before the process receives SIGTERM:
   ```yaml
   lifecycle:
     preStop:
       exec:
         command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"]
   ```

2. **`terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30`** on the PodSpec to give uWSGI enough time to finish in-flight requests after SIGTERM.

3. **Startup probe** with a generous failure threshold so slow first-boot scenarios (large DB, cold caches) do not trigger a liveness-kill loop:
   ```yaml
   startupProbe:
     httpGet:
       path: /v3
       port: 5000
     failureThreshold: 30
     periodSeconds: 10
   ```
   While the startup probe is active, kubelet skips both the liveness and readiness probes, preventing premature restarts.

**Motivation**: Without a preStop hook, there is a race condition between endpoint removal and process termination: kube-proxy may still route traffic to a pod that has already received SIGTERM. The 5-second sleep gives the cluster networking stack time to propagate the endpoint change. Without a startup probe, the liveness probe (15s initial delay, 20s period) can restart the container if Keystone takes longer than ~35 seconds to start — a realistic scenario after a schema migration or with cold Memcached caches. Together, these changes eliminate two classes of transient errors during rollouts.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` (`buildKeystoneDeployment`)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `keystone-api` container has a `preStop` lifecycle hook executing `sleep 5`
- [ ] PodSpec sets `terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30`
- [ ] `keystone-api` container has a startup probe on `/v3` port 5000 with `failureThreshold: 30` and `periodSeconds: 10`
- [ ] Existing liveness and readiness probes remain unchanged
- [ ] Unit tests verify the presence and values of the lifecycle hook, termination grace period, and startup probe
- [ ] Rolling update with `replicas >= 2` completes without dropping requests (verified manually or via integration test)

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/160

stories.title: Operator ensures graceful shutdown during rolling updates
stories.want: the Keystone API pods to drain in-flight requests before terminating during rolling updates
stories.so_that: end users do not experience dropped requests or 502 errors during deployments
criteria: keystone-api container includes a preStop lifecycle hook that executes /bin/sh -c 'sleep 5'
During a rolling update with replicas >= 2, kube-proxy has 5 seconds to remove the endpoint before SIGTERM is sent to the process
uWSGI has up to 25 seconds (30s grace - 5s sleep) to finish in-flight requests after receiving SIGTERM

stories.title: Operator ensures Keystone survives slow cold starts
stories.want: the Keystone API container to have a startup probe with a generous failure threshold
stories.so_that: slow first-boot scenarios (large DB migrations, cold caches) do not trigger liveness-kill loops
criteria: keystone-api container has a startupProbe using httpGet on /v3 port 5000
startupProbe has failureThreshold: 30 and periodSeconds: 10, allowing up to 300 seconds for startup
While the startup probe is active, kubelet skips both liveness and readiness probes
After the startup probe succeeds, liveness and readiness probes resume with their existing configuration

stories.title: Existing probe behavior remains unchanged
stories.want: the existing liveness and readiness probes to remain unchanged
stories.so_that: steady-state health checking behavior is not regressed
criteria: LivenessProbe still uses httpGet /v3 port 5000 with initialDelaySeconds: 15 and periodSeconds: 20
ReadinessProbe still uses httpGet /v3 port 5000 with initialDelaySeconds: 5 and periodSeconds: 10
Existing unit tests for liveness and readiness probes continue to pass without modification

stories.title: Unit tests verify all graceful shutdown configuration
stories.want: comprehensive unit tests covering the lifecycle hook, termination grace period, and startup probe
stories.so_that: regressions to graceful shutdown behavior are caught before merge
criteria: Unit test verifies preStop hook command is [/bin/sh, -c, sleep 5]
Unit test verifies startupProbe path, port, failureThreshold, and periodSeconds
All tests use existing Gomega assertion patterns from reconcile_deployment_test.go

stories.title: Integration test verifies graceful shutdown config in full reconciliation
stories.want: integration tests to verify the graceful shutdown fields survive a full reconciliation cycle
stories.so_that: the deployment spec is correctly persisted to the API server with all lifecycle fields
criteria: Integration test creates a Keystone CR and drives full reconciliation
Resulting Deployment has preStop lifecycle hook, terminationGracePeriodSeconds, and startupProbe

requirements.description: The keystone-api container SHALL have a preStop lifecycle hook that executes sleep 5 via /bin/sh -c
requirements.rationale: A 5-second sleep before SIGTERM gives kube-proxy and iptables time to propagate endpoint removal, preventing traffic from being routed to a terminating pod

scenarios.name: PreStop hook is present on keystone-api container
scenarios.then: the keystone-api container has a Lifecycle.PreStop.Exec handler

scenarios.name: PreStop hook uses exec handler not httpGet
scenarios.then: PreStop.Exec is non-nil and PreStop.HTTPGet is nil
and_then: the exec command has exactly 3 elements

requirements.description: The PodSpec SHALL set terminationGracePeriodSeconds to 30
requirements.rationale: The default Kubernetes terminationGracePeriodSeconds is 30, but setting it explicitly documents the intent and prevents future changes to the default from affecting behavior. The 30-second window gives 5s for preStop sleep + 25s for uWSGI to drain requests.

scenarios.name: TerminationGracePeriodSeconds is explicitly set
scenarios.then: PodSpec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds is a non-nil pointer to int64(30)

scenarios.name: Value is sufficient for preStop + request drain
scenarios.then: it is greater than the preStop sleep duration (5s)
and_then: the remaining time (25s) is sufficient for uWSGI to finish in-flight requests

requirements.description: The keystone-api container SHALL have a startup probe on /v3 port 5000 with failureThreshold 30 and periodSeconds 10
requirements.rationale: The startup probe allows up to 300 seconds for Keystone to initialize (e.g., after a schema migration or with cold caches), during which kubelet skips liveness and readiness probes to prevent premature restarts

scenarios.name: Startup probe is configured with correct endpoint
scenarios.then: StartupProbe uses httpGet with path /v3 and port 5000

scenarios.name: Startup probe has generous failure threshold
scenarios.when: the startup probe configuration is inspected
and_then: the maximum startup time is 300 seconds (30 * 10)

scenarios.name: Startup probe does not set initialDelaySeconds
scenarios.then: initialDelaySeconds is 0 (default) since the startup probe itself handles the startup window

requirements.description: Existing liveness and readiness probes SHALL remain unchanged
requirements.rationale: The startup probe addition must not alter existing health check behavior during steady-state operation

scenarios.name: Liveness probe is unchanged
scenarios.then: LivenessProbe uses httpGet /v3 port 5000, initialDelaySeconds 15, periodSeconds 20
and_then: all existing liveness probe assertions pass without modification

scenarios.name: Readiness probe is unchanged
scenarios.then: ReadinessProbe uses httpGet /v3 port 5000, initialDelaySeconds 5, periodSeconds 10
and_then: all existing readiness probe assertions pass without modification

requirements.description: Unit tests SHALL verify the presence and values of the lifecycle hook, termination grace period, and startup probe
requirements.rationale: Regression protection for graceful shutdown configuration using existing test patterns

scenarios.name: Unit test covers preStop hook
scenarios.then: it asserts the preStop lifecycle hook command is ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"]

scenarios.name: Unit test covers startup probe
scenarios.then: it asserts startupProbe path, port, failureThreshold, and periodSeconds

requirements.description: Integration tests SHOULD verify graceful shutdown fields survive full reconciliation
requirements.rationale: The envtest integration tests verify that the deployment spec is correctly persisted to the API server, catching issues that unit tests with fake clients cannot

scenarios.name: Full reconciliation produces correct deployment spec
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created and reconciled to completion via envtest
scenarios.then: the resulting Deployment has preStop hook, terminationGracePeriodSeconds, and startup probe with correct values

tasks.title: Add preStop lifecycle hook, terminationGracePeriodSeconds, and startup probe to buildKeystoneDeployment (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go, modify buildKeystoneDeployment to: (1) Add Lifecycle field to keystone-api container with PreStop exec handler running ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"], (2) Add TerminationGracePeriodSeconds: ptr.To(int64(30)) to PodSpec, (3) Add StartupProbe with httpGet /v3 port 5000, failureThreshold 30, periodSeconds 10. Existing liveness and readiness probes must remain unchanged. Import k8s.io/utils/ptr if not already imported.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for preStop hook, terminationGracePeriodSeconds, and startup probe (REQ-005)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go, extend TestReconcileDeployment_DeploymentSpec to add assertions for: (1) container.Lifecycle.PreStop.Exec.Command equals ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"], (2) deploy.Spec.Template.Spec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds equals ptr.To(int64(30)), (3) container.StartupProbe.HTTPGet.Path equals /v3 and Port equals 5000, (4) container.StartupProbe.FailureThreshold equals 30 and PeriodSeconds equals 10. Follow existing Gomega assertion style with comment blocks (e.g. '// Verify startup probe (CC-0063).'). Existing probe assertions must continue passing.

tasks.title: Add integration test for graceful shutdown deployment spec (REQ-006)
tasks.description: In operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go, add TestIntegration_GracefulShutdownSpec that: (1) creates a Keystone CR and drives full reconciliation using existing helpers (setupEnvTestWithController, createPrerequisites, driveFullReconciliation), (2) fetches the resulting Deployment, (3) asserts keystone-api container has preStop lifecycle hook with command ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"], (4) asserts PodSpec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds is 30, (5) asserts startupProbe has correct httpGet, failureThreshold, and periodSeconds. Follow existing integration test patterns (TestIntegration_PDBSpec is a good reference).

test_specifications.story: Unit tests verify all graceful shutdown configuration
Unit tests verify all graceful shutdown configuration
Unit tests verify all graceful shutdown configuration
test_specifications.expected: container.StartupProbe is non-nil, uses httpGet /v3 port 5000, failureThreshold 30, periodSeconds 10
Existing liveness probe assertions (httpGet /v3 port 5000, initialDelaySeconds 15, periodSeconds 20) pass without modification
Existing readiness probe assertions (httpGet /v3 port 5000, initialDelaySeconds 5, periodSeconds 10) pass without modification

test_specifications.story: Integration test verifies graceful shutdown config in full reconciliation
test_specifications.expected: After full reconciliation via envtest, resulting Deployment has preStop hook, terminationGracePeriodSeconds 30, and startup probe with correct values

review_criteria: All SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-005) have passing unit tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go
Existing liveness and readiness probe assertions pass without modification (no regression)
preStop hook command is exactly ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"] — no additional arguments or different shell
terminationGracePeriodSeconds is explicitly set as a pointer (not zero-value), using ptr.To(int64(30)) or equivalent
startupProbe uses httpGet (not tcpSocket or exec) matching the existing probe endpoint /v3 port 5000
golangci-lint passes with no new warnings on modified files
go vet passes on the keystone operator module
Integration test TestIntegration_GracefulShutdownSpec follows existing envtest patterns and covers all three new fields
implementation_notes:
This is a small, focused change to a single function (buildKeystoneDeployment) in reconcile_deployment.go. The three additions are:

1. **Lifecycle.PreStop** on the container: `corev1.Lifecycle{PreStop: &corev1.LifecycleHandler{Exec: &corev1.ExecAction{Command: []string{"/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 5"}}}}`

2. **TerminationGracePeriodSeconds** on PodSpec: `TerminationGracePeriodSeconds: ptr.To(int64(30))` — note this uses k8s.io/utils/ptr which is already a project dependency (used in integration_test.go).

3. **StartupProbe** on the container: follows the same httpGet pattern as existing probes but with failureThreshold and periodSeconds instead of initialDelaySeconds.

No new imports are needed in the production code beyond possibly `k8s.io/utils/ptr`. The test file already imports everything needed.

**Pitfalls to avoid:**
- Do NOT modify liveness or readiness probes — they must remain exactly as-is
- terminationGracePeriodSeconds must be a *int64 pointer, not a bare int64
- The preStop exec command must be a string slice, not a single concatenated string
- startupProbe should NOT have initialDelaySeconds set (defaults to 0, which is correct since the probe itself handles the startup window)
